title: Coelogyne benguetensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne benguetensis is classified under the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This epiphytic orchid belongs to the genus Coelogyne, which comprises a large number of species. Within the genus, Coelogyne benguetensis is further grouped based on its characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. As a species of the Coelogyne genus, it shares similarities with other Coelogyne species. The exact classification of Coelogyne benguetensis at the species level is still a matter of ongoing research and study.

intro: Coelogyne benguetensis is a species of orchid native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which provide potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne benguetensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coelogyne benguetensis is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices.
